rows.headline: ICO calls for protections for 23andMe customer data
rows.title: ICO calls for protections for 23andMe customer data
rows.summary: ICO calls for protections for 23andMe customer data

rows.headline: Reprimand issued to Greater Manchester Police over CCTV failings
rows.title: Reprimand issued to Greater Manchester Police over CCTV failings
rows.summary: Police force demonstrated “serious shortcomings” in how they handle CCTV footage

rows.headline: Statement on cyber incidents impacting retailers
rows.title: Statement on cyber incidents impacting retailers
rows.summary: Statement on cyber incidents impacting retailers

rows.headline: London council reprimanded for exposing personal details of 6,528 people for almost two years
rows.title: London council reprimanded for exposing personal details of 6,528 people for almost two years
rows.summary: We have reprimanded the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham (the council) after it left exposed the personal information of 6,528 people for almost two years.

rows.headline: Sole trader fined £50,000 after making over 194,000 unlawful marketing calls
rows.title: Sole trader fined £50,000 after making over 194,000 unlawful marketing calls
rows.summary:
People receiving calls described them as “dishonest”, “threatening” and an “invasion of privacy” 
ICO investigation concludes sole trader failed to take reasonable steps to comply with the law

rows.headline: Compensation company fined £90,000 for unlawful marketing calls
rows.title: Compensation company fined £90,000 for unlawful marketing calls
rows.summary:
We have fined AFK Letters Co Ltd (AFK) £90,000 for making more than 95,000 unsolicited marketing calls to people registered with the Telephone Preference Service (TPS), in a clear breach of electronic marketing laws. 

AFK Letters is a company which writes letters seeking compensation and refunds for its customers.

rows.headline: Statement on British Library’s 2023 ransomware attack
rows.title: Statement on British Library’s 2023 ransomware attack
rows.summary:
In October 2023, the British Library reported a ransomware attack to us, which escalated because of the lack of multi-factor authentication on an administrator account.  

Following the incident, the British Library published a cyber incident review in March 2024, which provided an overview of the cyber-attack and key lessons learnt to help other organisations that may experience similar incidents

rows.headline: Law firm fined £60,000 following cyber attack
rows.title: Law firm fined £60,000 following cyber attack
rows.summary: We have fined Merseyside-based DPP Law Ltd (DPP) £60,000, following a cyber attack that led to highly sensitive and confidential personal information being published on the dark web.

rows.headline: Statement from the ICO on data protection complaint response times
rows.title: Statement from the ICO on data protection complaint response times
rows.summary: "Anyone who has felt the need to make a complaint to us deserves a timely response. Our current response times are not where we want them to be, and we know how frustrating this is for people who are asking for our help.

rows.headline: Transparency must be the default position for water companies
rows.title: Transparency must be the default position for water companies
rows.summary: Public interest in water companies is higher than ever, with firms across the UK facing scrutiny over sewage discharges into lakes, rivers and the sea. Concerns about sewage pollution are mounting, as people have no choice but to rely on these companies.

rows.headline: Statement on police use of facial recognition technology (FRT)
rows.title: Statement on police use of facial recognition technology (FRT)
rows.summary: Facial recognition technology (FRT) can bring clear benefits in helping to prevent and detect crime, but it relies on processing large amounts of sensitive personal data. Therefore, its use must be necessary and proportionate, and its design must meet expectations of fairness and accuracy.

rows.headline: Review into use of children’s data by financial services
rows.title: Review into use of children’s data by financial services
rows.summary: More can be done by the financial services sector when dealing with children’s data.

rows.headline: John Edwards speaks at IAPP's Data Protection Intensive UK 2025
rows.title: John Edwards speaks at IAPP's Data Protection Intensive UK 2025
rows.summary: Hello and thank you for having me at IAPP London. I’ve seen that Sir Chris Bryant is also speaking here today. I was grateful to Chris for speaking at our much smaller event last November – what came through to me there, as it does now, is his understanding of how privacy and data protection affects people. The positives and the harms.

rows.headline: Statement on O'Carroll vs Meta
rows.title: Statement on O'Carroll vs Meta
rows.summary: "People have the right to object to their personal information being used for direct marketing, and we have been clear that online targeted advertising should be considered as direct marketing. "

rows.headline: Package of measures unveiled to drive economic growth
rows.title: Package of measures unveiled to drive economic growth
rows.summary: Today (Monday 17 March) we have set out a raft of new measures that support the Government’s growth agenda.

rows.headline: Statement on 23andMe investigation
rows.title: Statement on 23andMe investigation
rows.summary: Statement from Stephen Bonner, ICO Deputy Commissioner - Regulatory Supervision.

rows.headline: Software provider fined £3m following 2022 ransomware attack
rows.title: Software provider fined £3m following 2022 ransomware attack
rows.summary: We have fined Advanced Computer Software Group Ltd (Advanced) £3.07m for security failings that put the personal information of 79,404 people at risk.

rows.headline: Protecting children’s data in a digital world
rows.title: Protecting children’s data in a digital world
rows.summary: Part of parenting is educating and preparing your child for daily life, from showing them how to cross the road safely to teaching them stranger danger. When that daily life also involves social media and video sharing platforms – with children spending up to eight hours a day on these channels – safety is no less significant. But it feels more complicated.

rows.headline: Information Commissioner’s updated response to the Data (Use and Access) (DUA) Bill
rows.title: Information Commissioner’s updated response to the Data (Use and Access) (DUA) Bill
rows.summary: Information Commissioner’s updated response to the Data (Use and Access) (DUA) Bill

rows.headline: Investigations announced into how social media and video sharing platforms use UK children’s personal information
rows.title: Investigations announced into how social media and video sharing platforms use UK children’s personal information
rows.summary: ICO investigating how TikTok uses 13–17-year-olds' personal information to make recommendations to them. Regulator also announces it is investigating how Reddit and Imgur assess the age of their child UK users

rows.title: Pre-deployment evaluation of Anthropic’s upgraded Claude 3.5 Sonnet

rows.headline: Our strategy for levelling the playing field for online tracking in 2025
rows.title: Our strategy for levelling the playing field for online tracking in 2025
rows.summary: Online tracking is a part of everyday digital life. It enables personalised advertising, funds many free services and shapes our online experiences.

rows.headline: Company fined £200,000 for instigating unlawful loan promotion nuisance texts
rows.title: Company fined £200,000 for instigating unlawful loan promotion nuisance texts
rows.summary: We have fined West Sussex-based company ESL Consultancy Services Ltd (ESL) £200,000 for knowingly instigating unlawful loan promotion nuisance text messages being sent to people who had not consented to receive them.

rows.headline: ICO response to government on economic growth
rows.title: ICO response to government on economic growth
rows.summary: The Prime Minister, the Chancellor, and the Business Secretary wrote to the Information Commissioner (along with 16 other regulators) at the end of December asking for proposals to boost business confidence, improve the investment climate, and foster sustainable economic growth.

rows.headline: Debunking data protection myths about AI
rows.title: Debunking data protection myths about AI
rows.summary: From unlocking medical breakthroughs to revolutionising public services in the UK, AI has huge potential to transform our lives for the better. Across the economy, businesses seem excited about investing in AI to drive growth and innovation, while the tech is inspiring vibrant discussions amongst policymakers and the public alike.

rows.headline: ICO takes action to tackle cookie compliance across the UK’s top 1,000 websites
rows.title: ICO takes action to tackle cookie compliance across the UK’s top 1,000 websites
rows.summary: We have today announced plans to bring the UK’s top 1,000 websites into compliance with data protection law.
